# AGENTS.md — Generated by Niyam v0.1.0
# Tool: kiro | Generated: 2026-07-02
# Modules: nextjs, typescript, tailwind, prisma, shadcn, fullstack, clean-code, tdd, performance, git, ci-cd, testing

---

## Stack: Next.js
- Use App Router with server components by default; client components only for interactivity.
- Colocate loading.tsx, error.tsx, and page.tsx in each route segment.
- Use next/image for all images. Never use raw <img> tags.
- Fetch data in server components; pass to client components as props.

## Stack: TypeScript
- Enable strict mode. Never use `any` — use `unknown` + type guards.
- Export explicit return types on public functions.
- Prefer discriminated unions over optional fields for variant state.

## Stack: Tailwind
- Use class variance authority (cva) for component variants.
- Never mix Tailwind utilities with custom CSS unless unavoidable.
- Use design tokens via tailwind.config.ts — no hardcoded colors or spacing.

## Stack: Prisma
- Run `prisma generate` after every schema change. Use transactions for multi-model writes.
- Never expose raw Prisma client in API routes — wrap in a data access layer.

## Stack: shadcn/ui
- Import components from @/components/ui. Never modify shadcn source directly.

## Role: Full-Stack
- Own features end-to-end: schema → API → UI → test.
- Keep business logic in a service layer, not in route handlers or components.
- Validate at boundaries: API input, form submission, environment variables.

## Principles: Clean Code
- Names reveal intent. No abbreviations unless domain-standard.
- Functions do one thing. If you need "and" to describe it, split it.
- No duplication. Extract, don't copy-paste.

## Principles: TDD
- Write the failing test first. Never write production code without a failing test.
- Each test communicates one behavior. Name it after the behavior, not the method.
- Refactor only on green. Never change tests and code in the same step.

## Principles: Performance
- Measure before optimizing. No premature optimization without profiling data.
- Lazy-load below-the-fold components and non-critical JS.
- Use React.memo/useMemo only when measured — not prophylactically.

## Workflow: Git
- Commits: conventional format (feat:, fix:, chore:). Subject under 72 chars.
- Branch per feature. Rebase onto main before merge.
- Never commit generated files, .env, or node_modules.

## Workflow: CI/CD
- All PRs must pass: lint, typecheck, test, build.
- Deploy previews for every PR. Production deploy only from main.

## Workflow: Testing
- Unit tests for pure logic. Integration tests for API routes and DB queries.
- Use MSW for HTTP mocking. Tests must be deterministic — no network reliance.
